story.iWant: a servlet filter that extracts tenant_id from JWT and populates TenantContext
story.soThat: tenant context is available for all subsequent processing (Layer 1)
story.tasks: - AC1: TenantContextFilter.kt created as @Component with @Order(Ordered.HIGHEST_PRECEDENCE + 10) - AC2: Filter extracts tenant_id from JWT claim (after JWT validation in Epic 3) - AC3: TenantContext.set(tenantId) populates ThreadLocal - AC4: Missing tenant_id claim rejects request with 400 Bad Request - AC5: Filter ensures cleanup in finally block (TenantContext.clear()) - AC6: Integration test validates tenant extraction from real Keycloak JWT - AC7: Metrics emitted: tenant_context_extraction_duration, missing_tenant_failures

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. TenantContextFilter.kt created as @Component with @Order(Ordered.HIGHEST_PRECEDENCE + 10) 2. Filter extracts tenant_id from JWT claim (after JWT validation in Epic 3) 3. TenantContext.set(tenantId) populates ThreadLocal 4. Missing tenant_id claim rejects request with 400 Bad Request 5. Filter ensures cleanup in finally block (TenantContext.clear()) 6. Integration test validates tenant extraction from real Keycloak JWT 7. Metrics emitted: tenant_context_extraction_duration, missing_tenant_failures

doc.relevance: Defines Layer 1 of 3-layer tenant isolation: JWT extraction and ThreadLocal population
doc.snippet: Layer 1: TenantContextFilter (Servlet Filter) - Runs after Spring Security JWT validation (Epic 3) - Extracts tenant_id from validated JWT claims - Populates TenantContext ThreadLocal - Missing tenant_id → 400 Bad Request (fail-closed) - Cleanup in finally block prevents ThreadLocal leaks - @Order(Ordered.HIGHEST_PRECEDENCE + 10) ensures proper execution sequence

doc.relevance: Epic 3 JWT validation provides authenticated JWT with tenant_id claim
doc.snippet: Epic 3 Layer 4: Claims Schema Validation validates required claims (sub, iss, aud, exp, iat, tenant_id, roles). JWT validation runs BEFORE TenantContextFilter in filter chain. Spring Security populates SecurityContextHolder with authenticated JWT. Filter pattern: try { extract + populate } finally { cleanup } to prevent leaks.

doc.section: Spring Boot Test Configuration - Filter Stack
doc.relevance: Story 3.10 pattern for filter order and Spring Security integration
doc.snippet: Story 3.10: Filter-Stack vs MVC-Stack investigation (9.5 hours). Pattern: Filters run in Filter-Stack BEFORE Spring Security. @Order annotation controls filter execution sequence. TenantContextFilter must run AFTER Spring Security JWT validation. Use @Order(Ordered.HIGHEST_PRECEDENCE + 10) for post-security filters.
Layer 1: Extract tenant_id from JWT and populate request context. Fail-closed design: missing tenant_id rejects request immediately.

Story 4.2: TenantContextFilter - Layer 1 Tenant Extraction - Filter extracts tenant_id from JWT claim (after JWT validation in Epic 3) - Missing tenant_id claim rejects request with 400 Bad Request - Filter ensures cleanup in finally block (TenantContext.clear()) - Metrics emitted: tenant_context_extraction_duration, missing_tenant_failures

artifact.reason: Story 4.1 TenantContext API for setCurrentTenantId() and clearCurrentTenant()
Epic 3 JWT validation patterns for extracting claims from authenticated JWT
Epic 3 Keycloak integration test patterns with Testcontainers
Base class for Keycloak integration tests with JWT generation

constraints.constraint: Filter must run AFTER Spring Security JWT validation (Epic 3). Use @Order(Ordered.HIGHEST_PRECEDENCE + 10) to ensure proper sequence. Spring Security runs at Ordered.HIGHEST_PRECEDENCE. TenantContextFilter runs at HIGHEST_PRECEDENCE + 10 (after security, before business logic).

Missing tenant_id claim must reject request with 400 Bad Request (fail-closed). Do NOT continue request processing without tenant context. Clear, generic error message (CWE-209 protection): "Missing required tenant context".
Filter MUST use finally block for cleanup: TenantContext.clear(). Prevents ThreadLocal leaks in pooled thread environments. Cleanup happens even if exception occurs during filter processing.
Extract tenant_id from JWT.getClaimAsString("tenant_id"). JWT is already validated by Epic 3 (10-layer validation). Assume JWT is valid and authenticated when filter runs.
Emit Micrometer metrics: tenant_context_extraction_duration (timer), missing_tenant_failures (counter). Use MeteredTokenValidator pattern from Epic 3 Story 3.9 (Template Method Pattern).

interface.name: Spring Servlet Filter
interface.description: Standard Spring Boot servlet filter with @Component and @Order annotations. Implements jakarta.servlet.Filter interface.

interface.name: JWT Claim Extraction
interface.description: Extract tenant_id from authenticated JWT in SecurityContextHolder. JWT is already validated by Epic 3 Spring Security configuration.
interface.pattern: val authentication = SecurityContextHolder.getContext().authentication as? JwtAuthenticationToken val jwt = authentication?.token val tenantId = jwt?.getClaimAsString("tenant_id") ?: throw ResponseStatusException(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, "Missing required tenant context")

interface.name: Micrometer Metrics
interface.description: Timer for filter execution duration, counter for missing tenant failures. Follow Epic 3 Story 3.9 Template Method Pattern for metrics.
interface.metrics: tenant_context_extraction_duration (Timer) - Filter execution time missing_tenant_failures (Counter) - Count of missing tenant_id failures

standards.standard: Constitutional TDD: Test-first development mandatory. Integration test BEFORE filter implementation (Red-Green-Refactor).

Use Kotest FunSpec with @SpringBootTest for integration tests. Pattern from Epic 3: @Autowired field injection + init block (NOT constructor injection). Plugin order: id("eaf.testing") BEFORE id("eaf.spring-boot") in build.gradle.kts.
Use Testcontainers for real Keycloak JWT generation. Follow Epic 3 KeycloakIntegrationTest pattern for JWT creation. Real JWTs ensure tenant_id claim extraction is production-realistic.

Validate filter runs AFTER Spring Security (order verification). Test missing tenant_id → 400 Bad Request response. Test successful extraction → TenantContext populated. Test cleanup → ThreadLocal cleared after request.

ideas.idea: Test AC2: Extract tenant_id from real Keycloak JWT (use KeycloakIntegrationTest base)
Test AC3: Verify TenantContext.getCurrentTenantId() returns extracted tenant_id
Test AC4: Missing tenant_id claim → 400 Bad Request with error message
Test AC5: ThreadLocal cleanup - verify context cleared after request completes
Test AC6: Integration test with real Keycloak JWT containing tenant_id claim
Test AC7: Verify metrics emitted (tenant_context_extraction_duration timer incremented)
Test filter order: verify TenantContextFilter runs AFTER Spring Security JWT validation
Test exception during filter processing → cleanup still happens (finally block)
Test concurrent requests → each request has isolated tenant context (no cross-contamination)
